subject: Economics
chapter: Balance of Payments and Foreign Exchange

concepts.title: Current Account and Capital Account
concepts.description: The Current Account (CA) and Capital Account (KA) are two components of the Balance of Payments (BOP). The Current Account represents the transactions related to goods, services, income, and transfers, while the Capital Account represents the transactions related to long-term capital, financial investments, and changes in reserves.

concepts.title: Recording of BOP
concepts.description: The Balance of Payments is recorded using the Double-Entry Bookkeeping System, where each transaction is recorded twice - once as a credit and once as a debit.

concepts.title: Method of Presenting BOP
concepts.description: The Balance of Payments can be presented in a Summary Balance Sheet format, which includes the Current Account, Capital Account, and Reserve Account.

concepts.title: Current Account Items
concepts.description: Mnemonic Device: GICSI - Goods, Income, Current Services, Income (to remember the items included in the Current Account)

concepts.title: Confusion between CA and KA
concepts.description: Students often confuse the Current Account with the Capital Account, but it's essential to remember that the Current Account includes transactions related to goods, services, income, and transfers, while the Capital Account includes transactions related to long-term capital, financial investments, and changes in reserves.

concepts.title: Types of Exchange Rates
concepts.description: There are two types of exchange rates: Nominal Exchange Rate and Real Exchange Rate. Nominal Exchange Rate represents the current market exchange rate, while Real Exchange Rate represents the exchange rate adjusted for inflation.

concepts.title: Balance of Payments Account
concepts.description: The Balance of Payments Account is a statistical statement that summarizes all the economic transactions of a country with the rest of the world over a specific period, typically a year.

concepts.title: Impact of BOP on Economy
concepts.description: A country with a persistent deficit in the Balance of Payments may lead to a decline in its currency value, increased inflation, and reduced economic growth, while a surplus may lead to an appreciation of the currency value, reduced inflation, and increased economic growth.
